title: cephalophilon capitatum
intro: Cephalophilon capitatum, also known as the Headed Headache Plant, is a type of flowering plant native to South Africa, characterized by its unique, rounded clusters of small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the sesquiterpene lactones present in its leaves and flowers. Consuming Cephalophilon capitatum has been reported to provide various health benefits, including relief from headaches, fever reduction, and improved digestion, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using it in combination with other herbs in a tincture or capsule form.
